validateStep runs AFTER the automatic input validation.
Use it for business rules beyond field validation.
showcase.component.wizard.sections.autoV: The wizard validates inputs before advancing. By default, previous steps and the immediate next step are also clickable in the stepper, reusing the same validation as the Continue button.
showcase.component.wizard.sections.befor: Async validation before moving forward (e.g. API call).
showcase.component.wizard.sections.stepN: Use stepNavigation to limit clickable steps. The default is previous-and-next; this example only allows clicking previous steps.

showcase.component.wizard.title: SgWizard
showcase.home.about.li1: Less boilerplate: no need to write Controller for each field.
showcase.home.about.li2: Validation and default messages are built in.
showcase.home.about.li4: Easier to reuse layouts and rules across screens.
showcase.home.about.p1: SG components wrap React Hook Form. When you provide {name} and {control}, the component applies {controller} internally.
showcase.home.about.p2: You can still use the component without RHF (as a controlled input), but with {name} and {control} it becomes an RHF wrapper.
showcase.home.about.title: About
showcase.home.description: Interactive catalog of all components in {pkg}. Click any component to see interactive examples and prop docs.
showcase.home.i18n.p1: Components use pt-BR by default and are ready for other languages via i18n. Keys follow the {namespace} namespace.
showcase.home.i18n.p2: To change the global language, wrap your app with the package provider:
showcase.home.i18n.p3: If you are not using React at the config point (or want a simple global setup), use:

Optional icon rendered in the body. If omitted, info/warning/danger use default icons.
Visual accent and default confirm button style.
Enables the top severity accent line in the dialog.
Custom customColor and elevation for the dialog card.
Custom label, icon, severity, and button appearance.
Callbacks fired on cancel and confirm.
Defines whether it closes automatically after each action.
ESC and overlay close behaviors inherited from dialog.

Column definitions (header, field, body, sort, filter).
Unique key field for row identity and selection.
Total records in the backend (used with lazy).
Initial row index of the page (controlled mode).
Sort direction: asc, desc, or none.
Shows column filter inputs in header.
Callback fired when filters are applied.
Message displayed when there are no records.
Imperative API: clearFilters, clearSelection, resetSort, goToPage.
showcase.common.playground.description: Simulate the main props in real time.
showcase.common.playground.description.w: Simulate the main props of {component} in real time.
showcase.component.timeProvider.title: SgTimeProvider
showcase.component.timeProvider.subtitle: Server-synchronized time provider. Prevents clock drift by anchoring time to the server timestamp.
showcase.component.timeProvider.sections: Wrap your app with SgTimeProvider and consume useSgTime to get server-synchronized time.
useSgTime works without a provider using Date.now() as fallback. There is no server synchronization.
Recommended pattern: initialize SgTimeProvider in the root layout with a server-side timestamp.
showcase.component.componentsI18nProvide: Configures the locale and messages for all SeedGrid components in the subtree.
Wrap your application with SgComponentsI18nProvider and pass locale and messages.
Change locale at runtime. Components re-render automatically with the new messages.
Alternative to the Provider. Useful for SSR or pre-mount initialization.
Override only the keys you need. Merge your overrides with a built-in messages object.
showcase.component.clockThemeProvider.su: Distributes SgClock themes throughout the component tree via context.
showcase.component.clockThemeProvider.se: Wrap SgClock and SgClockThemePicker with SgClockThemeProvider to share theme resolution.
Pass a themes array to the provider to inject local themes without global registration.
Use registerTheme to register themes globally. They are available to all SgClockThemeProvider instances.
showcase.hook.persistentState.title: useSgPersistentState
showcase.hook.persistentState.subtitle: React state that automatically persists and rehydrates from localStorage or a configured strategy.
showcase.hook.persistentState.sections.b: Works without a provider using localStorage directly. Reload the page to see the value persist.
showcase.hook.persistentState.sections.n: Wrap components with SgEnvironmentProvider to isolate state per user or tenant via namespace.
showcase.hook.persistentState.sections.s: Use serialize and deserialize to persist complex types like Date or custom objects.
showcase.hook.persistentState.sections.v: Increment stateVersion in SgEnvironmentProvider to invalidate previously persisted data.
showcase.hook.persistence.title: useSgPersistence
showcase.hook.persistence.subtitle: Manual access to the persistence operations (load, save, clear) from the configured strategy.
showcase.hook.persistence.sections.basic: Use load, save, and clear to manually manage persistence. The key is resolved using namespace and scope from the environment.
Use useSgPersistentState for automatic sync. Use useSgPersistence when you need manual control over when data is read or written.
showcase.hook.environment.title: useSgEnvironment
showcase.hook.environment.subtitle: Reads the current environment context: namespace provider, persistence strategy, scope, mode, and state version.
showcase.hook.environment.sections.defau: Without SgEnvironmentProvider, the hook returns default values: empty namespace, localStorage strategy, and app:unknown scope.
showcase.hook.environment.sections.provi: Inside SgEnvironmentProvider the hook reads the configured namespace, scope, mode, and stateVersion.
showcase.hook.time.title: useSgTime
showcase.hook.time.subtitle: Returns server-synchronized time that ticks every second. Consumes SgTimeProvider context.
showcase.hook.time.sections.basic.title: Basic usage
showcase.hook.time.sections.basic.descri: Use nowMs() to get the current timestamp and tick to trigger re-renders every second.
showcase.hook.time.sections.fallback.des: Without SgTimeProvider, the hook falls back to Date.now(). Components still work but without server synchronization.
showcase.hook.time.sections.tick.title: Using tick for re-renders
showcase.hook.time.sections.tick.descrip: The tick property increments every second. Use it in dependency arrays to trigger effects or derived values.
showcase.hook.componentsI18n.title: useComponentsI18n
showcase.hook.componentsI18n.subtitle: Returns the current locale and messages configured by SgComponentsI18nProvider.
showcase.hook.componentsI18n.sections.ba: Use useComponentsI18n to read the active locale and translate keys using the t helper.
showcase.hook.componentsI18n.sections.cu: Combine useComponentsI18n with t to build components that automatically respect the active locale.
showcase.component.whistler.subtitle: Contextual inline messaging with an imperative API (sgWhistle) and a host that stays inside the layout flow.
All examples below dispatch into this host, so the feedback stays contextual and pushes content instead of overlaying it.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.bas: Mount one SgWhistler where the layout should reveal contextual messages. Then dispatch whistles from anywhere in the page.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.rou: rounded defaults to true (rounded-lg). Set rounded to false for square corners. Dispatch a whistle to compare both stacks.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.sev: The service exposes convenience methods by severity, while host props such as max, newestOnTop and gap control the stack.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.act: Whistles can expose quick CTAs and lightweight visual emphasis without needing overlay positioning.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.upd: Loading flows are usually created once and then updated in place as the task resolves or fails.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.pro: The helper centralizes loading, success and error state transitions for async flows.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.cus: Use sgWhistle.custom for arbitrary JSX and customColors on the host to align the stack with the surrounding context.
showcase.component.whistler.sections.pla: Interactive sandbox for host props and common whistle flows.
showcase.component.whistler.props.max: Maximum visible whistles rendered by the host.
showcase.component.whistler.props.newest: Reverses the visual order of the visible stack.
showcase.component.whistler.props.classN: Styles the host container in the page flow.
showcase.component.whistler.msg.severity: Inline feedback rendered inside the layout flow.

showcase.component.whistleHost.subtitle: Contextual inline messaging with an imperative API (sgWhistle) and a host that stays inside the layout flow.
showcase.component.whistleHost.livePrevi: All examples below dispatch into this host, so the feedback stays contextual and pushes content instead of overlaying it.
Mount one SgWhistleHost where the layout should reveal contextual messages. Then dispatch whistles from anywhere in the page.
The service exposes convenience methods by severity, while host props such as max, newestOnTop and gap control the stack.
Whistles can expose quick CTAs and lightweight visual emphasis without needing overlay positioning.
Loading flows are usually created once and then updated in place as the task resolves or fails.
The helper centralizes loading, success and error state transitions for async flows.
Use sgWhistle.custom for arbitrary JSX and customColors on the host to align the stack with the surrounding context.
Interactive sandbox for host props and common whistle flows.

showcase.component.whistleHost.props.new: Reverses the visual order of the visible stack.

showcase.component.toaster.subtitle: Notification system with imperative API (toast) and renderer (SgToaster).
showcase.component.toaster.props.duratio: Default duration in ms for auto-close.
showcase.component.toaster.props.closeBu: Shows close button on each toast.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.base: In this showcase, SgToaster is already mounted in layout. In a real app, mount it once at root.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.load: Store returned id and reuse it to switch state without creating another toast.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.prom: Convenience helper for loading/success/error lifecycle in promises.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.acti: Use action for quick CTA and custom for free-form render.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.opti: Each toast accepts duration, closeButton, className, and style.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.cust: Example focused only on custom colors per type.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.roun: rounded defaults to true (rounded-md). Set rounded to false for square corners - it applies to the whole toaster. Toggle it live in the Playground above.
showcase.component.toaster.sections.play: Here you can change all props in real time (position, duration, visibleToasts, closeButton, richColors, transparency and customColors).
showcase.component.toaster.msg.loading.s: This toast stays open until you update or close it.
showcase.component.toaster.msg.action.un: The item returned to the list.
showcase.component.toastHost.props.durat: Default duration in ms for auto-close.
showcase.component.toastHost.props.close: Shows close button on each toast.
showcase.component.toastHost.msg.loading: This toast stays open until you update or close it.
The item returned to the list.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.lo: Store returned id and reuse it to switch state without creating another toast.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.pr: Convenience helper for loading/success/error lifecycle in promises.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.ac: Use action for quick CTA and custom for free-form render.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.op: Each toast accepts duration, closeButton, className, and style.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.cu: Example focused only on custom colors per type.
showcase.component.toastHost.subtitle: Marks where toasts appear in the tree. When multiple hosts exist simultaneously, the deepest one takes priority - letting a page override the layout host automatically.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.wh: Smart version of SgToaster with priority support between multiple instances in the React tree.
showcase.component.toastHost.whatIs.p1: - renders toasts where it is placed, with no priority logic.
showcase.component.toastHost.whatIs.p2: - same as SgToaster, but registers globally. If two hosts exist in the tree at the same time, the deepest one (closest to the current content) is active. The layout host is automatically disabled while the page host is mounted.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.ba: In this showcase, SgToastHost is already mounted in the page. In a real app, mount it once at root.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.pr: React runs children effects before parents (depth-first postorder). The page host registers last - and last registered wins.
showcase.component.toastHost.priority.st: Layout mounts -> the layout SgToastHost registers (stack: [layout]). It becomes active.
Page mounts -> the page SgToastHost registers (stack: [layout, page]). It becomes the active one.
User navigates -> the page SgToastHost unmounts, leaves the stack. Layout becomes active again.
A new page with its own host mounts -> it becomes active immediately.
showcase.component.toastHost.positioning: Accepts the same 6 positions as SgToaster.
showcase.component.toastHost.sections.pl: Change all props in real time (position, duration, visibleToasts, closeButton, richColors, transparency and customColors).
showcase.component.multiSelect.subtitle: Select-style multi-select. The trigger shows a chevron that turns into a check when open; the dropdown lists options with checkboxes and stays open while you toggle.
1) Basic (closed / open, with and without selection)
Click the field to open. Toggle multiple options; the list stays open.
searchable enables a search field at the top of the dropdown.
clearable adds the clear button; maxSelected caps the number of items.
With enabled={false} the field neither opens nor changes the selection.

showcase.component.multiSelect.rows.summ: Labels shown before the "N selected" summary.
showcase.component.multiSelect.rows.plac: Text shown when nothing is selected.
showcase.component.multiSelectChips.subt: Multi-select variant where selected items become removable chips in the trigger. The checkbox dropdown and search are the same as SgMultiSelect.
showcase.component.multiSelectChips.sect: Click the field to open. Each selected item becomes a chip with a remove button.
searchable enables a search field at the top of the dropdown.
clearable adds the clear button; maxSelected caps the number of items.
With enabled={false} the field neither opens nor removes chips.
showcase.component.multiSelectChips.rows: Shows a search field in the dropdown.
Text shown when nothing is selected.

showcase.component.fadeDigit.section7Des: Interactive clock with configurable color palette and fontSize.

showcase.component.stringAnimator.header: Animates one string into another, character by character. Supports six styles: roller3d, flip, neon, fade, discard, and matrix. Fully configurable for speed, alignment, and colors.
showcase.component.stringAnimator.sectio: Animates "LUCIANO" into "MARTA". Extra positions are padded on the right (alignTo="left").
Animates "42" into "1337". Padding is applied on the left (alignTo="right") and digits animate from right to left.
Each character uses SgNeonDigit with a neon glow effect. Use color and backgroundColor for customization.
Each character uses SgFadeDigit: the current value fades out and the next one fades in.
Each character uses SgDiscardDigit: the previous value is discarded with an outgoing motion.
Each character uses SgMatrixDigit with a 5x7 LED matrix rendering. Great for dashboards and digital panels.
With autoStart=true, animation starts automatically whenever targetString changes. Works with all styles.
Comparison of velocity=10, 50, and 90 using the same animation.
showcase.component.stringAnimator.playgr: Configure all props in real time, including the new styles and colors.

showcase.component.clockThemePreview.sec: Compact previews of built-in themes for visual comparison.
showcase.component.clockThemePreview.pro: Visual theme used to render the preview.
Additional classes applied to the SVG.
showcase.component.linearGauge.subtitle: Linear gauge with axis, ranges, bar pointer, marker pointers, and drag support.
showcase.component.linearGauge.section1D: Colored ranges with a controllable primary pointer.
showcase.component.linearGauge.section2D: Vertical orientation with two draggable pointers.
showcase.component.linearGauge.props.val: Primary pointer value and change event.
showcase.component.linearGauge.props.pri: Primary pointer settings (shape, color, size, draggable).
showcase.component.linearGauge.props.bar: Filled bar up to current value.
showcase.component.playground.subtitle: Playground with code editor + preview, supporting renderBody and appFile.
showcase.component.playground.section1De: When interactive=false, it works as a collapsible code block.
showcase.component.playground.section2De: Receives only JSX body and auto-builds an example App.
showcase.component.playground.example2De: Use Run to refresh the preview.
showcase.component.playground.section3De: When codeContract=appFile, you control the full App.tsx file.
showcase.component.playground.section4De: Example without external card and without resize/expand.
showcase.component.playground.propHeader: Header shown in the playground card.
showcase.component.playground.propDefaul: Imports injected automatically in renderBody mode.
showcase.component.radialGauge.subtitle: Radial gauge with ranges, pointers, and ring thickness control (ringThickness).
showcase.component.radialGauge.section1D: Primary needle pointer with warning ranges.
showcase.component.radialGauge.section2D: Example with thick ring and no visual clipping using ringThickness={36}.
showcase.component.radialGauge.section3D: With isAxisInversed, custom angles, and animate={false}.
showcase.component.radialGauge.section4D: Combines marker, range pointer, and value-based annotation.
showcase.component.radialGauge.section5D: Donut-style visual using a range-type pointer.
showcase.component.radialGauge.section6D: Example with width/height, axisWidth, animationDuration, primaryPointerLabel, labelFormatter and ariaLabel.
showcase.component.radialGauge.section7D: Try pointerType, ringThickness, inverted axis, and ticks/labels visibility.
showcase.component.radialGauge.props.val: Primary pointer value and change event.
showcase.component.radialGauge.props.axi: Main ring style, thickness and center content.
showcase.component.skeleton.subtitle: Visual placeholder for loading states with shape, size, and animation support.
showcase.component.skeleton.section2Desc: Use text shape with different widths to simulate real lines.
showcase.component.skeleton.section4Desc: Loading example for a card (image + title + text).
showcase.component.skeleton.section5Desc: Common placeholder compositions for data screens.
showcase.component.skeleton.section6Desc: Playground to simulate shape, size, radius and animation in real time.
showcase.component.periodSelector.header: Preset period picker that resolves a temporal rule into a real date interval.
showcase.component.periodSelector.sectio: Default flags: current and past groups, plus custom period.
All groups enabled, including All and future periods.
Select Custom period to enter a start and end date range.
The resolved interval is clamped to the min/max bounds (here limited to the current month).
Fired with the preset and resolved date interval.
Enables the current periods group (THIS_*, today, yesterday).
Enables the future periods group (tomorrow, NEXT_*). Default true.
Enables the All option (no date filter). Default true.
Enables the custom interval option with a date range.
Locale used for labels and formatting.
Lower bound applied as a clamp to the resolved interval.
Upper bound applied as a clamp to the resolved interval.
showcase.component.periodSelector.sectio: Toggle the permit flags and options and see the resolved period in real time.
Toggle searchable, clearable and the selection limit in real time.
showcase.component.multiSelectChips.sect: Toggle searchable, clearable and the selection limit in real time.
